headline: Rwanda confirmation cycle. Composite 27.3 sustained — sub-threshold delta of 0.0. CPJ January 2026 UNHRC submission documents ongoing journalist imprisonment, surveillance, espionage charges, and enforced disappearance; Rwanda's RSF 2026 Press Freedom rank remains 139th. Opposition leader Victoire Ingabire's June 2025 arrest on criminal-group charges still active. Retired Brigadier General Frank Rusagara died in prison after a decade of incarceration. Rwanda's continued M23 support in eastern DRC remains documented. No structural change in window. Mandatory re-queue cleared from April 16 pending change proposal — current scoring confirmed against documented evidence base. Developing band sustained.
key_evidence: January 2026: CPJ submitted report to UNHRC ahead of Rwanda's Universal Periodic Review documenting ongoing journalist imprisonment, surveillance, espionage charges against journalists, and enforced disappearance.
Rwanda ranks 139th of 180 in RSF 2026 Press Freedom Index (declining trajectory from prior years).
Opposition leader Victoire Ingabire arrested June 2025 on criminal-group charges; case ongoing.
Retired Brigadier General Frank Rusagara died in prison after a decade of incarceration without consistent release of medical records.
Rwanda's continued military intervention in eastern DRC supporting M23 documented through HRW 2026 and UN Group of Experts reporting.
HRW World Report 2026 confirms continuing pattern of opposition suppression.

evidence.finding: Press freedom submission to UNHRC documenting journalist imprisonment, surveillance, and enforced disappearance.
Documented patterns of opposition suppression, journalist imprisonment, M23 support in DRC.

scoring_rationale.AWR: 2.25 unchanged. CPJ and HRW reports cataloguing pattern previously scored.
scoring_rationale.BND: 2.25 unchanged. M23 support in DRC documented but already factored.
scoring_rationale.ACC: 1.5 unchanged. UPR process is the operative external accountability — already scored.
scoring_rationale.INT: 1.5 unchanged. Public commitments to media freedom contradicted by documented imprisonment pattern — already scored.

math_check.validator_check: Zero composite delta; Developing band sustained.

negativeSignals: CPJ-documented journalist imprisonment continuing; RSF rank 139; Ingabire arrest active; Rusagara prison death; M23 support in eastern DRC; HRW-documented opposition suppression.
positiveSignals: Universal Periodic Review process underway provides external accountability mechanism; Rwanda has not blocked UNHRC engagement.
sector_context: Rwanda is the only major Africa-region governance entity at boundary-proximate composite (27.3) with both positive (post-genocide reconstruction, IT-economy growth) and negative (opposition suppression, regional intervention) signals. M23 cluster cross-references DRC scoring.
open_actions_resolved: April 16 pending change proposal: cleared as confirmation
open_actions_carried: Watch: any structural change in DRC posture (ceasefire, M23 disengagement)
